commit.message:
feat: domain-based org auto-assignment on registration

- Add domain field to Organization model
- First user from a domain creates org (ADMIN), subsequent users join (VIEWER)
- Update seed to set domain on demo org

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix paste, add Shift+Enter newline, Option+Backspace word-delete, and app name in top bar

- Fix Cmd+V paste in terminal by reading NSPasteboard directly via objc
- Add paste_text() helper to deduplicate bracketed paste logic
- Shift+Enter sends newline (\n) instead of carriage return
- Option+Backspace sends Ctrl+W (backward-kill-word) instead of ESC+DEL
- Always show "StickyTerminal" centered in the top bar

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[feat](trx-backend): VirtualChannelManager trait + SdrVirtualChannelManager impl

Add VirtualChannelManager trait in trx-core::vchan with types VChannelInfo,
VChanError, and SharedVChanManager alias. Re-export from trx-backend::vchan.

Implement SdrVirtualChannelManager in trx-backend-soapysdr:
- Wraps Arc<SdrPipeline> + shared AtomicI64 center_hz
- add_channel / remove_channel / set_channel_freq / set_channel_mode
- Slot-stability: on remove, shifts pipeline_slot for surviving channels
- update_center_hz: recomputes IF offsets for all virtual channels on retune
- update_primary_meta: keeps channel-0 freq/mode in sync for API consumers

Wire into SoapySdrRig (holds Arc<SdrVirtualChannelManager>, exposes
channel_manager()), SdrPipeline (shared_center_hz AtomicI64), and RigHandle
(vchan_manager: Option<SharedVChanManager>). main.rs extracts the manager
before boxing the SDR rig and stores it in the handle.

Add max_virtual_channels to SdrConfig (default 4, TOML-configurable).
Add 5 unit tests: add, remove, permanent guard, cap, out-of-bandwidth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

commit.message:
fix: TotalSupply 1B AET (1Q µAET); per-agent SDK keypairs

- genesis: TotalSupply 1T→1Q µAET (1M→1B AET); all six bucket allocations
  scaled ×1000; OnboardingPoolTotal 300B→300T; onboarding tiers ×1000
  (tier1: 50M→50B, tier2: 10M→10B, tier3: 1M→1B, tier4: 100K→100M)
- ledger/supply: BaseSupply 1T→1Q µAET; comment updated
- genesis_test: tier boundary assertions updated to new amounts
- integration/e2e_test: alloc constant and comment updated
- sdk/python: add generate_keypair() and register_with_keypair() to
  AetherNetClient; all four agent scripts use per-agent Ed25519 keypairs
  so each worker receives its own onboarding allocation; task_poster
  budgets reduced to fit within spendable balance; 3 unit tests added

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add discovered sources feature — scan Ultrix to find unnamed sources

Polls all 288 Ultrix destinations via the bridge, cross-references with
existing ultrix_sources DB entries, and shows unnamed sources with an
"Add to Matrix" button. Includes search/filter and inline label input.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(multi-window): add multi-window support with Cmd+N

Add support for opening multiple independent editor windows within a single
app instance. Each window gets its own ViewModel and ImageEditorService.
Slideshow mode is restricted to one window at a time. Quitting via Cmd+Q
with multiple windows open prompts for confirmation.

App.axaml.cs:
- Replace single ViewModel with per-window creation via CreateNewWindow()
- Set ShutdownMode.OnLastWindowClose for automatic exit on last window close
- Add quit confirmation dialog when multiple windows are open (Cmd+Q only)
- MacOS "Open With" now opens a new window instead of loading into existing
- About dialog finds the active MainWindow instead of using desktop.MainWindow

MainWindow.axaml / MainWindow.axaml.cs:
- Add "New Window" menu item and Cmd+N / Ctrl+N keyboard shortcut
- Add Cmd+N to help dialog keyboard shortcuts list

MainWindowViewModel.cs:
- Enforce single-window slideshow via App.ContinuousModeOwner static property
- Reset to first image in list when sort order is changed
- Clear continuous mode ownership on Dispose

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Factory Dashboard — full pipeline operations UI

Implements complete authenticated dashboard for the Agentic Harness pipeline:
- Supabase Auth (email/password + magic link) with middleware protection
- Kanban board with real-time Supabase Realtime subscriptions
- Issue detail page with stage timeline, markdown body, agent runs
- Metrics page with throughput chart and stage duration averages
- Settings page for tracked repos and admin user management
- GitHub sync API (fetches issues, extracts station/complexity labels)
- API routes: /api/issues, /api/sync, /api/metrics/*, /api/runs
- Dark mode (#0A0A0A), DM Sans headings, Inter body, JetBrains Mono
- Stage color system consistent across all components
- DB migration: dash_issues, dash_stage_transitions, dash_agent_runs,
  dash_dashboard_config, dash_user_roles with RLS policies

commit.message:
Production hardening: security, permissions, audit log, mobile UX, docs

- Server-side permission enforcement via role_permissions table (middleware/permissions.ts)
- Centralized overlap check (utils/overlapCheck.ts) replacing 4 duplicates
- Audit log writes for all reservation mutations (utils/auditLog.ts)
- Error Boundary in App.tsx to prevent total crash
- Global rate limiting (100 req/min) + login (5 req/min)
- WCAG AA contrast fix for all status colors
- Password minimum 8 chars validation
- Client-side midnight crossing detection and duration warnings
- Partial composite index for availability overlap queries (migration 005)
- Mobile: date nav + view toggle moved to header, timeline fills 100% height
- Mobile timeline: fixed header/column layout (only grid body scrolls)
- Mobile agenda: filter out zones with 0 tables, seasonal zone filtering
- Timeline endpoint: join zones for is_active + seasonal date filtering
- Permissions admin panel (PermissionsManager component)
- Comprehensive project documentation (docs/DOCUMENTATION.md)

commit.message:
fix(auth): skip 401 redirect for login endpoint so wrong-password error displays

The api.js response interceptor was catching all 401s and hard-redirecting
to /login, which prevented the 'Invalid credentials' error from ever showing
on the login form. Now only redirects for authenticated routes, not auth endpoints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pending additions never being deployed or cleared

Three related bugs:
1. computeFinalAllocations received investmentAmount without pending_addition,
   giving new bots (investment_amount=0) zero weight in multi-bot merging
2. totalInvested only used currentBotValue, ignoring totalPendingAdditions —
   new capital from 'Adding $X' badges was never deployed into trades
3. pending_addition was never reset to 0 after execution, so the 'Adding'
   badge would persist forever and capital would be double-counted next cycle

Fix: pass investmentAmount + pendingAddition to allocator, include pending
additions in totalInvested, and absorb pending_addition into investment_amount
after each successful execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: security, correctness, and observability fixes from dual-agent review

Security:
- Remove 'change-me' token signing fallback; throw in production
- Use timingSafeEqual for admin password comparison
- Remove Admin/Owner Portal links from public landing page nav
- Validate OAuth callback source field against allowlist
- Fix .env.example: add missing vars, remove duplicate, remove personal email

Correctness:
- Fix timezone bug in AI slot display (was showing UTC on Vercel)
- Fix 'Invalid Date' in confirmation emails (use parsedDt.toISOString())
- Parameterize 'barbershop' in system prompt (support all business types)
- Change tool dispatch if→else if (prevent silent overwrite)
- Fix customerPhone||customerEmail fallback (no email in phone field)
- Add 'cancelled' status to BookingRecord, exclude from conflict checks
- Add date filter to listBookings (future bookings only)
- Add sendOwnerBookingNotification to widget booking route
- Fix FAQ to match actual cancellation flow
- Replace fake social proof stats with honest metrics
- Fix chat route error status: 500 for server errors, 400 for client

Observability:
- Log parseArgs failures, getAvailableSlots errors, email send failures
- Check response.ok in alerts.ts and log failures
- Alert operator when billing row is missing (was silent block)
- Expand isOffTopic terms for non-barbershop businesses

cleanup: remove dead artifacts (#21)

* cleanup: remove dead artifacts

- src/cleanup_generated_workouts.py: one-off migration script with no importers
- src/config/constants.py: zero importers; all defined symbols were unused
- src/config/logging_config.py: zero importers; setup_logging/get_logger never called

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ta27zUTa8bUe5Vt4yQCN1F

* cleanup: rename garmin_sync to garmin_fetcher

Rename src/garmin_sync.py → src/garmin_fetcher.py and update all
import sites, script references, and tests. Rename only; no logic
changes to the module itself.

Updated references:
- bin/sync_garmin_data.sh: script invocation path
- bin/delete_duplicate_workouts.py: bare import via sys.path
- src/workout_uploader.py: dynamic import in get_garmin_client
- tests/test_garmin_sync.py: all from-imports (7 occurrences)
- skills/garmin_sync.py: docstring comment (accuracy)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ta27zUTa8bUe5Vt4yQCN1F

* cleanup: simplify discord bot report and state handling

1. Remove dead supplemental_workout_details parsing block from sync
   command — variable was populated but never consumed; auto-generation
   is disabled per CLAUDE.md.

2. Extract duplicated MOTD/ANSI-strip logic into _strip_motd() helper
   (module-level _ANSI_ESCAPE and _MOTD_KEYWORDS constants); replace
   both copies in report_command and morning_report_task.

3. Replace blocking subprocess.run() in /report command with
   await run_coach_cli() — same command, same 120s timeout, same error
   handling; fixes event-loop blocking during report generation.

Goal 4 (_handle_vdot_confirm sqlite → helpers) skipped: get_state uses
a read-write connection and auto-creates the DB file; current code uses
read-only mode with an explicit db_path.exists() guard — behaviors
differ, not safe to swap without semantic change.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ta27zUTa8bUe5Vt4yQCN1F

* cleanup: share active session helper

Both src/morning_report.py and src/daily_workout_formatter.py defined an
identical _get_active_sessions_safe() — a 7-line wrapper that calls
skills.plans.get_active_sessions() and catches all exceptions to return [].

Add get_active_sessions_safe(db_path=None) as a public function in
skills/plans.py (the canonical home for plan-retrieval logic), then replace
both local definitions with:

    from skills.plans import get_active_sessions_safe
    get_active_sessions = get_active_sessions_safe  # patchable alias for tests

The import is placed after each file's sys.path setup block to preserve
the existing project-root path initialisation order. The module-level
get_active_sessions alias is kept so existing test patches
(morning_report.get_active_sessions, daily_workout_formatter.get_active_sessions)
continue to work without modification.

Workout label constants (_WORKOUT_TYPE_LABELS vs _LABELS) were not
centralised: morning_report.py includes "rest": "Rest Day" while
daily_workout_formatter.py does not, so the two dicts serve different
purposes.

44/44 targeted tests pass (no change from baseline).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ta27zUTa8bUe5Vt4yQCN1F

* cleanup: share llm repair constants

Two pieces of knowledge were duplicated across brain/planner.py and
brain/macro_plan.py:

1. The fix-JSON prompt text ("The JSON you returned failed schema
   validation. Return ONLY a corrected JSON object…") was defined as
   _FIX_JSON_PROMPT in planner.py and written inline as an f-string in
   macro_plan.py. Move to FIX_JSON_PROMPT in brain/llm.py (the module
   that owns all LLM-calling logic) and import in both callers.

2. The Haiku model ID "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" was a raw string at
   planner.py:580; macro_plan.py had it in a local constant _MACRO_MODEL.
   Add MODEL_HAIKU to brain/llm.py alongside the existing MODEL constant,
   then reference it from both files (macro_plan.py keeps its _MACRO_MODEL
   alias so no further call sites need touching).

_extract_or_reprompt was NOT shared: planner.py's version uses the
default model (Sonnet) for its format reprompt, while macro_plan.py's
inline version forces _MACRO_MODEL (Haiku). That difference is
intentional and merging would silently change runtime behaviour.

Syntax verified with py_compile. Test baseline unchanged: 308 passed /
49 pre-existing pydantic failures (environment issue, not introduced here).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ta27zUTa8bUe5Vt4yQCN1F

* cleanup: dedupe planner day helpers

Two inner helpers were defined identically inside two separate module-level
functions (_enforce_structure_constraints and replan_remaining_week):

  _to_rest(day_obj, flag)      — byte-for-byte identical in both functions
  _weekday / _weekday_name     — same strftime expression, different argument
                                 style (_weekday_name took day_obj and extracted
                                 .date; _weekday took the iso string directly)

Lift both to module level as _weekday(iso_day) and _to_rest(day_obj, flag),
in a new "Shared day-mutation helpers" section just before
_enforce_structure_constraints. Update the six _weekday_name(d) call sites
in _enforce_structure_constraints to _weekday(d.date).

Not extracted:
  _is_run    — captures run_types from the enclosing scope (local set, not
               a module constant); single instance, no dedup benefit
  _is_hard   — different predicate (HARD_TYPES vs run_types); single instance
  _make_easy — sets intent and uses "optional" priority; differs from _to_easy
  _to_easy   — "nice_to_have" priority, no intent field; single instance

py_compile: OK. Planner/macro tests require pydantic (not installed in this
environment) — pre-existing blocker, unrelated to this change.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ta27zUTa8bUe5Vt4yQCN1F

* cleanup: map metrics storage paths

Add test_metrics_path_map.py as Track F mapping/validation-prep.

Pins the reader/writer contracts for both the legacy 'metrics' blob
table (upsert_metrics/get_metrics_range) and the typed 'daily_metrics'
table (upsert_daily_metrics/get_daily_metrics), and adds parity tests
comparing _rollup_readiness (JSON path) vs _rollup_readiness_from_sqlite

No schema migration, no deletions, no behavioral changes.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ta27zUTa8bUe5Vt4yQCN1F

Replace get_metrics_range (legacy blob table) with get_daily_metrics
(typed daily_metrics table) in on_readiness_change.run().

The only field read from the legacy row was training_readiness; the

Update test_on_readiness_change.py to patch memory.db.get_daily_metrics
instead of memory.db.get_metrics_range at all seven call sites, and

All test_on_readiness_change.py tests remain blocked by the pre-existing

Behavior equivalence verified via four direct logic tests covering:

Remove the upsert_metrics call from hooks/on_sync.py now that

commit.message:
fix(runner): auto-trigger cleaner after scraper finishes

Scraper and cleaner ran on independent cron schedules, so raw data
could sit uncleaned for hours. Now runner.sh chains the cleaner
immediately after every scraper run (success or failure).

commit.message:

feat(mcp): add aggregate statistics and outlier detection for fuzz results (#287)

* feat(fuzzer): add aggregate statistics and outlier detection to fuzz results

Add job-level aggregate statistics (status code distribution, body length
and timing distribution stats) and outlier detection to fuzz_results query.
Outliers are detected by status code (differs from most frequent),
body length (outside median +/- 2 stddev), and timing (outside median
+/- 2 stddev). Add outliers_only filter to return only outlier results.

- Add GetFuzzResultRawStats to FuzzStore for efficient stats computation
- Add OutliersOnly/OutlierIDs to FuzzResultListOptions for SQL filtering
- Restructure summary to include total_results, status_code_distribution,
  body_length stats, timing_ms stats, and outlier result IDs
- Update MCP help documentation with new summary format and examples
- Add comprehensive unit tests for statistics, outlier detection, and filters

Refs: USK-278

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(mcp): add aggregate statistics and outlier detection for fuzz results

Enhance the fuzz_results query resource with detailed aggregate statistics
and outlier detection to help AI agents efficiently identify anomalies in
large fuzz result sets.

Changes:
- Replace basic summary (avg/total duration) with comprehensive statistics:
  status_code_distribution, body_length and timing_ms distributions
  (min/max/median/stddev)
- Add outlier detection: by_status_code (different from most frequent),
  by_body_length and by_timing (outside median +/- 2 sigma)
- Add outliers_only filter to return only outlier results
- Update MCP tool description and help content

The computation is done in the MCP handler layer, keeping the store
interface unchanged. Statistics use population stddev and median-based
outlier detection for robustness.

Refs: USK-278

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- F-1: pass outlier IDs to SQL query via ResultIDs in FuzzResultListOptions
  for correct pagination with outliers_only filter
- F-2: use full-precision float64 (rawDistribution) for outlier boundary
  detection, only round when building output distributionStats
- F-3: deterministic baseline selection with numeric tiebreaker when
  multiple status codes have equal count

Refs: USK-278

commit.message:
Architectural cleanup: remove dead code, debug artifacts, and unused services

Phase 1 - Security & debug artifacts:
- Delete cookies.txt (live session token in version control)
- Delete debug-auth.html (dev-only test page)
- Delete debug_openstreetmap.js and .cjs (dev test scripts)

Phase 2 - Dead backend servers:
- Delete flask_backend/ (unused Python/Flask Amadeus wrapper duplicating Express routes)
- Delete server/auth-server.ts (unused standalone auth server on port 3001)
- Delete start-auth-server.sh (orphaned launch script)

Phase 3 - Unused services:
- Delete server/kayakScrapingService.ts (1025 lines, never imported or called)
- Delete server/geonamesService.ts (optional fallback, GEONAMES_USERNAME never set)
- Delete server/apiNinjasService.ts (optional fallback, API_NINJAS_KEY never set)
- Remove their imports and conditional fallback blocks from locationService.ts

Phase 4 - Dead travel tips feature:
- Remove travel_tips and user_tip_preferences tables from initdb.sql
- Remove all storage methods (getTravelTips, createTravelTip, seedTravelTips,
  getUserTipPreferences, createOrUpdateUserTipPreferences, dismissTipForUser)
- Remove internal row types and mapper functions from storage.ts
- Remove TravelTip, InsertTravelTip, TravelTipWithDetails, UserTipPreferences,
  InsertUserTipPreferences, UserTipPreferencesWithUser types from shared/schema.ts

Phase 5 - Harden debug pages:
- Wrap /amadeus-test and /location-database routes in isDevelopment guard in App.tsx
  so they are inaccessible in production builds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: audit — test coverage + complexity elimination

Phase 1: Test coverage (+32 tests)
- validation.rs: 15 tests for all 6 public functions (was 0)
- routing.rs: 8 edge case tests (non-numeric, array Contains, type mismatch)
- orchestrator.rs: 5 tests (has_session, cleanup_stale, check_bounds paths)
- interrupts.rs: 4 error path tests (invalid resolve/cancel, multi-interrupt)

Phase 2: Complexity elimination
- Consolidate bounds checking: Envelope::check_bounds() as single source of truth,
  at_limit() becomes thin wrapper — deleted redundant free function
- Remove terminated state duplication: PipelineSession.terminated removed,
  envelope.bounds.terminated is sole authority (5 write sites unified)
- Extract Kernel::process_agent_result(): eliminates fragile PCB reconciliation
  arithmetic (saturating_sub/max(0) → direct delta application)

Post-audit improvements:
- Test fixtures: empty_ctx()/outputs_with() in routing, test_response() in interrupts
- #[derive(Default)] on AgentExecutionMetrics (compiler-offloaded construction)
- Eliminated metrics.clone() via scalar extraction before ownership transfer

276 unit tests + 17 integration tests pass, cargo clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[consensus] Remove randomness fast path code (#18870)

* [consensus] Remove randomness fast path

The randomness fast path used a separate, higher secrecy threshold
(via ConfigV2) for faster randomness reconstruction. It was disabled
by default and the mainnet on-chain config was reverted from V2 to V1.
This removes all fast path runtime code while preserving backward
compatibility with BCS-serialized on-chain and local DB state.

Key decisions:
- Transcripts.fast and AugmentedData.fast_delta Option fields are
  retained (always None) for BCS deserialization compatibility
- Move ConfigV2 struct is kept (published on-chain type) but new_v2()
  is removed
- Key pair deserialization handles old format gracefully by falling
  through to regenerate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix formatting and rebuild cached packages

Apply cargo +nightly fmt and rebuild aptos-cached-packages after
removing new_v2() from randomness_config.move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Return error on key pair deserialization failure instead of regenerating

Regenerating a new key pair on deserialization failure could cause a
mismatch with stale CertifiedAugData already persisted in storage,
leading to incorrect randomness shares. Instead, return an error to
disable randomness for the epoch, which is the safer behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep old key pair format for DB backward compatibility

Instead of migrating to a new serialization format, keep the old
(AugKeyPair, Option<AugKeyPair>) format and always write None for
the fast path key pair. This avoids any migration complexity and
ensures existing DB entries remain readable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: keep rounding and Move code unchanged for safety

Per review feedback:
- Restore rounding code to original (pass None for fast path threshold
  instead of removing the parameter) to ensure replay safety since
  rounding is called inside VM during DKG transaction verification.
- Restore new_v2() in randomness_config.move since it's a published
  public function on the framework.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep DKGPvssConfig.fast_wconfig for BCS backward compatibility

Retain the fast_wconfig: Option<SSConfig> field in DKGPvssConfig
(always set to None) to preserve BCS serialization compatibility,
consistent with the approach taken for Transcripts.fast and
AugmentedData.fast_delta.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Fix tests from recent rebase that still referenced removed FastShare
type and old 4-argument verify() signature. Remove the fast share
sender verification test (no longer applicable) and update remaining
tests to use the new 3-argument verify().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in DKGPvssConfig PartialEq

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Keep ConfigV2 in OnChainRandomnessConfig enum for replay safety.
Historical DKG transactions may have used V2, and removing it from
TryFrom causes randomness_config_derived() to return None, which
would produce different DKGRounding parameters and break transcript
verification during blockchain replay.

V2 is now treated identically to V1: its secrecy_threshold and
reconstruction_threshold are used, but fast_path_secrecy_threshold
is ignored (fast path has been removed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Retain fast path secrecy threshold for rounding replay safety

Thread the fast_path_secrecy_threshold from on-chain ConfigV2 through
to DKGRounding::new, so that replay of historical V2-era DKG
transactions produces identical rounding parameters. For V1/Off
configs, None is passed (no fast path), preserving current behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Pass fast_wconfig from DKGRounding to DKGPvssConfig

Thread the fast_wconfig produced by DKGRounding through to
DKGPvssConfig instead of always using None. This ensures that
during replay of V2-era epochs, the DKGPvssConfig is constructed
with the same fast_wconfig as the original, matching the on-chain
BCS-serialized config exactly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Restore release builder V2 config and new_v2 constructor

Revert the removal of V2 variant from ReleaseFriendlyRandomnessConfig
and restore OnChainRandomnessConfig::new_v2() constructor. These are
needed for backward compatibility — removing them could break framework
upgrade compatibility checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in build_dkg_pvss_config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Remove unused lru dependency

The lru crate was only used for the fast path broadcast cache
(blocks_with_broadcasted_fast_shares) which has been removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Update Cargo.lock for lru removal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: modern-retro design overhaul (JetBrains Mono + wireframe style)

- Switch font to JetBrains Mono throughout
- Warm off-white background (#f5f0e8), almost-black text, deep red accent (#bf1a0a)
- Zero border-radius everywhere — sharp, geometric elements
- Wireframe buttons (.btn-wire) that invert to deep red on hover

topology: folded-cascode input + class-AB push-pull output

Folded cascode provides higher first-stage output impedance and
gain compared to simple mirror load, potentially improving CMRR
and reducing sensitivity to supply variations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: patch 7 security/precision bugs from audit

- Use parseUnits() instead of float math for swap amounts and gas fees
- Fix Gwei-to-wei conversion using parseUnits('gwei') instead of parseEther/divide
- Fix malformed TRON fallback RPC URL
- Add nginx security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)
- Prevent rate limiter IP spoofing via X-Forwarded-For
- Support variable decimal precision in amount validation

commit.message:
Add Instagram link to footer across all pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show all bulk-added properties in onboarding wizard (#182)

The bulk add loop called setHouses([...houses, newHouse]) where houses
was captured once at the start of the loop (stale closure). Each
iteration overwrote the previous, so only the last property appeared.
Switched to functional updater setHouses(prev => [...prev, newHouse])
so each iteration correctly appends to the latest state.

Closes #145

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

AdminLayout but was never committed, causing the Vercel build to fail
with "Module not found: Can't resolve '@/lib/logger'".

commit.message:
Fix infinite loop in delete branch modal

Remove `deleteModalBranch` from useEffect dependencies to prevent
infinite re-renders. The derived object reference changed on every
parent re-render, causing the merge check API call to loop endlessly.
Now the branch lookup happens inside the effect instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update skill for Astro 6+: add PRG pattern to Layer 1

Astro 6 stabilizes the Session API, enabling the PRG (Post-Redirect-Get)
pattern for Layer 1 form processing. This eliminates the browser's
"resubmit form?" warning on reload — strengthening Layer 1's "never
breaks" guarantee at the HTTP level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
